summary: Research the real 1763-1815 historical trajectory of each major power to script AI-autonomous event chains

beats.event: The Treaty of Paris ends the Seven Years' War disastrously for France, ceding New France (Canada) and all territory east of the Mississippi to Britain, transferring Louisiana to Spain, and reducing France in India to a handful of unfortified trading posts.
beats.gameplay_effect: At/near game start fire a scripted event that cedes the New_France/Canada region and eastern Mississippi provinces to GBR, cedes the Louisiana region to SPA (Spain), and cedes/reduces the Indian coastal provinces to GBR; apply a sharp prestige loss (-large) and a stability hit (-1 or -2) and add a war_exhaustion/treasury debt modifier to model the postwar fiscal ruin.

beats.event: Louis XV (b.1710, r.1715) dies of smallpox and is succeeded by his 19-year-old grandson Louis XVI (b.1754), inheriting a bankrupt treasury and a reform-resistant court.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a ruler-death event killing the current ruler and installing heir Louis XVI as king; apply a small legitimacy/stability wobble at succession and attach a persistent 'fiscal crisis' country modifier (monthly treasury drain / reduced income) that will not clear until the Revolution.

beats.event: France signs the Treaty of Alliance with the fledgling United States and enters the American Revolutionary War against Britain, seeking revenge for 1763.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire an AI-autonomous event that forms an alliance with USA and triggers declare_war on GBR; deepen the treasury-drain modifier (war spending) and grant a temporary prestige/aggressive-stance boost so the AI commits to the anti-British war.

beats.event: The Treaty of Paris recognizes American independence; France gains only minor colonial scraps (Tobago, Senegal) while the war's cost pushes the monarchy toward terminal bankruptcy.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a peace event ending the war with GBR, optionally granting a small colonial province (Senegal/Tobago), then break the USA wartime alliance to neutral and apply a severe treasury/debt modifier and stability penalty representing the unsustainable postwar deficit that sets up 1789.

beats.event: The Estates-General crisis erupts into revolution with the storming of the Bastille, shattering royal authority as the National Assembly seizes power.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a revolution-onset event that drops stability to near-minimum, spikes unrest/tyranny-equivalent, weakens the ruler's power, and arms a 'revolution' state flag; begin a countdown/decision chain toward government change while keeping Louis XVI nominally on the throne (constitutional-monarchy interim).

beats.event: France declares war on Austria (April) igniting the War of the First Coalition, then abolishes the monarchy and proclaims the First French Republic in September.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a two-part event: declare_war on AUS (Austria) and its ally PRU (Prussia), then execute a government change from monarchy to republic (republican government type); reset ruler to a republican head of state, apply a large prestige swing and aggressive-expansion posture so the AI wages the revolutionary war offensively.

beats.event: Louis XVI is tried by the Convention and guillotined, ending 1,000 years of French monarchy and rallying the crowned heads of Europe against the regicide Republic.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a ruler-execution event that kills Louis XVI (no heir succession — throne abolished), locks in the republican government, applies a stability shock plus a prestige/dread spike, and worsens opinion/adds casus belli with all monarchies (GBR, AUS, PRU, RUS, ESP) to model the widened coalition.

beats.event: General Napoleon Bonaparte (b.1769) overthrows the Directory in the coup of 18 Brumaire and becomes First Consul, concentrating power in himself.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a coup event that changes government from republic to a consulate/dictatorship type and installs a new ruler, Napoleon Bonaparte, with very high martial/stats; restore stability by +1/+2 and grant a prestige boost, ending the internal-unrest modifiers and re-orienting the AI toward outward conquest.

beats.event: Napoleon crowns himself Emperor of the French at Notre-Dame, transforming the Republic into the First French Empire.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a government-change event from consulate to empire/monarchy type with Napoleon as Emperor (found a new dynasty), apply a large prestige gain and a legitimacy/authority buff, and unlock aggressive expansion so the AI pursues the coming coalition wars.

beats.event: At Austerlitz Napoleon crushes the Austro-Russian armies of the Third Coalition; the following year he dissolves the Holy Roman Empire and organizes the Confederation of the Rhine under French protection, then smashes Prussia at Jena.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a conquest chain that wins the war against AUS and RUS, annexes/cedes German and Italian border regions to FRA, and creates client_state/vassal subjects out of the German minor tags (Confederation of the Rhine) plus a puppet in Italy/Naples; apply a big prestige gain and force PRU to cede territory after a follow-up defeat.

beats.event: Napoleon invades Russia with the Grande Armée; Moscow is taken but the catastrophic winter retreat annihilates the army and breaks French dominance.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire an event that triggers declare_war on RUS and initially advances, then a dated follow-up (late 1812) that destroys the bulk of French manpower/legions, applies a massive war_exhaustion and prestige/stability collapse, and flips subject and coalition loyalties (Confederation subjects begin defecting), setting up the 1813-14 collapse.

beats.event: The Sixth Coalition takes Paris; Napoleon abdicates and is exiled to Elba, the Bourbons are restored under Louis XVIII, and France is pushed back to roughly its 1792 borders — before Napoleon's 1815 return ends in final defeat at Waterloo (18 June) and permanent exile.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a collapse event that releases/frees all Confederation of the Rhine and Italian subjects, cedes back all conquered regions to AUS/PRU/RUS and their allies to restore pre-war borders, removes Napoleon as ruler and changes government back to Bourbon monarchy (install Louis XVIII); then a 1815.6.18 event models the Hundred Days' failure with a final prestige/stability nadir and Napoleon's permanent removal.

result.notes:
Key rulers: Louis XV (b.1710, acc.1715, d.1774.5.10, smallpox); Louis XVI (b.1754, acc.1774, executed 1793.1.21); Napoleon Bonaparte (b.1769, First Consul 1799.11.9, Emperor 1804.12.2, abdicated 1814.4.6, returned Mar-Jun 1815 "Hundred Days", d.1821 St Helena — outside mod window). Republican interregnum 1792-1799 had no single monarch (Convention/Directory).

Cross-power interactions the France file should coordinate with: (1) The three Partitions of Poland — 1772, 1793, 1795 — are joint PRU+AUS+RUS events (Poland-Lithuania, tag likely POL/PLC) that France does not participate in; script them in those powers' files, not FRA's, but they run concurrently with France's revolutionary wars. (2) Spain (SPA) is the recipient of Louisiana in 1763 and a French co-belligerent in the American war and again as a French client/ally under Napoleon (1808 intervention would be a later beat if extended). (3) Britain (GBR) is France's persistent antagonist across the whole arc (1763 victor, 1778-83 loser, then leader of every anti-French coalition through Waterloo). (4) The Confederation of the Rhine subjects (1806-1813) should be modeled as released German minor tags made into French client_states/vassals, then freed on the 1814 collapse.

Caveats: exact treaty-signing vs game-start (1763.2.16) means the Treaty of Paris cessions are best applied as the mod's 1763 starting map state or a turn-1 event rather than a mid-game declaration. Austerlitz/Jena/Confederation of the Rhine are compressed into one 1805-06 "conquest" beat for the 6-12 beat limit; a longer treatment could split Austerlitz (1805), Confederation of the Rhine (1806.7.12), Jena-Auerstedt (1806.10.14), and Tilsit (1807.7). The 1814 abdication and 1815 Waterloo are combined into the final beat for the same reason but carry distinct sub-dates.

beats.event: The Treaty of Paris ends the Seven Years' War with Britain victorious, confirming its acquisition of Canada (New France) and Florida and its supremacy over France in India after the collapse of French posts.
beats.gameplay_effect: On game-start (1763.2.16) grant GBR a large prestige and stability boost and ensure it holds the Canada and Bengal/Coromandel regions; break/deny any FRA alliance and set FRA as a rival to seed the coming wars.

beats.event: Parliament passes the Stamp Act, the first direct tax on the American colonies, igniting the 'no taxation without representation' protest movement.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a stability -1 shift for GBR and apply a rising unrest/discontent modifier to the Thirteen Colonies provinces; set a hidden colonial_unrest variable that ratchets toward revolt.

beats.event: The Boston Tea Party and subsequent Coercive (Intolerable) Acts escalate the colonial crisis toward open rebellion.
beats.gameplay_effect: Increment the colonial_unrest variable to its threshold, further reduce American-colony loyalty, and arm the trigger that will spawn the revolt in 1775.

beats.event: Fighting at Lexington and Concord opens the American Revolutionary War as the Thirteen Colonies rise against the Crown.
beats.gameplay_effect: start_civil_war / release the Thirteen Colonies as a breakaway USA tag at war with GBR; hit GBR with war-exhaustion and manpower strain, but keep Canada and India loyal.

beats.event: The Continental Congress adopts the Declaration of Independence, formally proclaiming the United States a sovereign nation.
beats.gameplay_effect: create_country / fully release USA as an independent tag (no longer a subject), set USA and GBR at war with a war-goal of independence, and give USA a diplomatic-recognition flag other powers can back.

beats.event: France signs the Treaty of Alliance with the United States and enters the war against Britain, joined by Spain (1779) and the Dutch Republic (1780), turning a colonial revolt into a global naval war.
beats.gameplay_effect: Have FRA form an alliance with USA and declare_war on GBR; queue ESP and NED to join the anti-GBR side; spike GBR war-exhaustion and stretch its fleets across multiple theatres.

beats.event: Cornwallis surrenders his army at Yorktown after a Franco-American siege, effectively deciding the war in America.
beats.gameplay_effect: Apply a heavy GBR prestige and war-score loss, destroy a GBR land stack in North America, and push the war-outcome resolution decisively toward USA independence.

beats.event: The Treaty of Paris recognizes United States independence; Britain loses the Thirteen Colonies but retains Canada, the Caribbean, and its Indian dominions.
beats.gameplay_effect: cede the Thirteen Colonies region to USA and end the war; apply a lasting GBR prestige penalty while explicitly preserving GBR control of Canada and India; return GBR to peace/consolidation posture.

beats.event: Revolutionary France declares war on Britain, opening more than two decades of near-continuous Anglo-French war (Wars of the Coalitions).
beats.gameplay_effect: Have FRA declare_war on GBR; GBR forms a First-Coalition alliance with AUS, PRU, and ESP and takes a war-goal against FRA; set GBR to fund coalition partners (subsidy/relation buffs).

beats.event: The Act of Union merges the Kingdoms of Great Britain and Ireland into the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland.
beats.gameplay_effect: change_country_tag / government-name change GBR to the United Kingdom, fully integrate/annex the Ireland territory into the core, and grant a modest prestige and stability boost.

beats.event: Nelson's fleet annihilates the combined Franco-Spanish navy at Trafalgar, securing unchallenged British naval supremacy for the rest of the era.
beats.gameplay_effect: Destroy the bulk of the FRA and ESP fleets, grant GBR a large prestige boost and a permanent naval-dominance modifier, and (via the Third Coalition) form alliances with AUS and RUS against FRA.

beats.event: With George III incapacitated by illness, his son George, Prince of Wales, becomes Prince Regent, exercising royal authority in the King's name.
beats.gameplay_effect: Install the heir as co_ruler/regent while George III remains the nominal monarch; apply a small legitimacy adjustment but no succession, keeping GBR's war policy against Napoleonic France unchanged.

beats.event: The Seventh Coalition crushes Napoleon at Waterloo, and the Congress of Vienna awards Britain the Cape Colony, Ceylon, and Malta, cementing its status as the dominant global and naval power.
beats.gameplay_effect: Form a Seventh-Coalition alliance (PRU, AUS, RUS, NED) and win the war against FRA; then annex/gain the Cape Colony, Ceylon, and Malta regions and grant GBR a major prestige and stability boost.

result.notes: Key ruler: George III (b. 1738, acceded 1760, r. through 1815 and beyond, d. 1820 — death is outside the 1763-1815 window so no succession event fires; model the 1811 Regency as a co-ruler/regent instead, with the future George IV as Prince Regent). Cross-power interactions: (1) The American war became a world war — FRA (1778), ESP (1779), and NED (1780) all joined against GBR; script them as sequential joiners, not a single declaration. (2) Spain flips sides over the period: a GBR enemy through the Revolutionary Wars but a GBR co-belligerent after 1808 in the Peninsular War once Napoleon invades it. (3) The Coalition alliances rotate membership (AUS, PRU, RUS, ESP, NED, POR) and repeatedly collapse and reform between 1793 and 1815 — GBR is the constant paymaster/coalition-builder via subsidies. (4) The three Partitions of Poland (1772, 1793, 1795) are joint PRU+AUS+RUS actions that do NOT involve GBR; only reference them if scripting those powers' arcs, not Britain's. (5) France's own arc (1789 Revolution, 1799 Napoleon's coup, 1804 Empire) should be owned by the FRA arc and merely hooked into GBR's war triggers. Caveat: exact treaty-signature days vs. ratification/news-arrival dates differ slightly in sources; dates given are the standard historical ones and can be nudged a few days for pacing without loss of accuracy.

beats.event: The Treaty of Hubertusburg ends the Seven Years' War on a status quo ante bellum basis, confirming Prussia's retention of Silesia against Austria and cementing its status as a European great power.
beats.gameplay_effect: At game start (1763.2.16) fire a white_peace resolution with AUS; lock PRU ownership+cores of the Silesia region (own_control_core), grant a large one-time prestige/legitimacy boost and a stability bump, and flag PRU as a great power. Set a standing AUS rivalry flag.

beats.event: The First Partition of Poland, coordinated by Prussia, Russia and Austria, gives Frederick II Royal (West) Prussia and Warmia, joining Brandenburg to East Prussia.
beats.gameplay_effect: Trigger a joint scripted annexation: PRU annexes/cores the West Prussia (Pomerelia/Warmia) states from POL via a partition wargoal or direct cede; simultaneously grant RUS and AUS their own POL states (global-var handshake so all three fire together). PRU prestige gain, POL stability drop and territory loss. No PRU alliance with POL.

beats.event: Frederick the Great forms the Fürstenbund (League of Princes) to rally the smaller German states and block Austrian expansion within the Holy Roman Empire.
beats.gameplay_effect: PRU forms alliances/guarantees with several minor German-state tags and reasserts the AUS rivalry (opinion penalty toward AUS, diplomatic-influence push into small HRE members). Modest prestige gain.

beats.event: Frederick II 'the Great' (b.1712, acc.1740) dies at Sanssouci; his nephew Frederick William II (b.1744) accedes.
beats.gameplay_effect: Ruler death event: kill the Frederick II character and set_ruler to Frederick William II with historical stats (weaker administration/martial). Small temporary stability/legitimacy dip during succession.

beats.event: The Second Partition of Poland, agreed by Prussia and Russia only (Austria excluded), gives Prussia Danzig, Thorn and Greater Poland (South Prussia).
beats.gameplay_effect: Joint PRU+RUS scripted annexation of further POL states (Posen/South Prussia, Danzig, Thorn) via partition wargoal/cede; global-var handshake with RUS only, AUS gets nothing this time. PRU territory+prestige gain, POL stability collapse.

beats.event: By the Treaty of Basel, Frederick William II makes a separate peace with Revolutionary France, quitting the First Coalition and establishing the neutrality of northern Germany for a decade.
beats.gameplay_effect: Force PRU out of the anti-French coalition: white_peace/break alliance with FRA and coalition partners, set a 'north German neutrality' flag that suppresses PRU joining wars against FRA until 1806. Minor prestige loss vs coalition members, stability stabilizes.

beats.event: The Third Partition erases Poland from the map; Prussia takes New East Prussia (Warsaw and Białystok) alongside Russian and Austrian shares.
beats.gameplay_effect: Final joint PRU+RUS+AUS scripted annexation that fully annexes/deletes POL; PRU cores the Warsaw/Białystok states. Large PRU territory gain; ensure POL tag is removed from play.

beats.event: Frederick William II dies; his son Frederick William III (b.1770) accedes and continues Prussian neutrality toward France.
beats.gameplay_effect: Ruler death event: kill Frederick William II, set_ruler to Frederick William III (cautious/low-martial stats). Reaffirm the neutrality flag; brief succession stability dip.

beats.event: Prussia belatedly joins the Fourth Coalition and is annihilated by Napoleon at the twin battles of Jena and Auerstedt, its army destroyed and Berlin occupied.
beats.gameplay_effect: Clear the neutrality flag and force PRU to declare_war on FRA (join Fourth Coalition). Then script a catastrophic defeat: destroy most PRU legions, French occupation of Brandenburg/Berlin, heavy war-exhaustion and stability/prestige crash.

beats.event: The Treaty of Tilsit strips Prussia of roughly half its territory — lands west of the Elbe form the Kingdom of Westphalia and the Polish partitions become the Duchy of Warsaw — leaving it a French-dominated rump.
beats.gameplay_effect: Force punitive peace: PRU cedes all states west of the Elbe (to WES/FRA client) and its Polish gains (to a new Duchy of Warsaw tag/RUS-FRA sphere); impose a large war indemnity (treasury/tribute), make PRU effectively a FRA client/tributary, big prestige+stability loss.

beats.event: The Stein–Hardenberg civil reforms and the Scharnhorst–Gneisenau military reforms (serfdom abolished 1807, army modernized under the Tilsit troop cap) rebuild the Prussian state.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a government-reform/modernization event: apply a lasting military-quality and administrative-efficiency modifier, gradual stability and manpower recovery, and civic-reform flags (serfdom abolition). Positive legitimacy trend under Frederick William III.

beats.event: After Napoleon's Russian disaster and the Convention of Tauroggen, Prussia signs the Treaty of Kalisz allying with Russia and declares the War of Liberation against France.
beats.gameplay_effect: Break the FRA client/alliance bond, form an alliance with RUS (and later AUS/GBR), and declare_war on FRA. Prestige/stability surge and manpower mobilization modifier for the national uprising.

beats.event: The Battle of Leipzig ('Battle of the Nations') is a decisive coalition victory that drives Napoleon out of Germany.
beats.gameplay_effect: Script a major coalition battle victory: liberate/re-occupy PRU home states, restore PRU legions and prestige, push the war frontline into France. Coalition alliance reinforced.

beats.event: At the Congress of Vienna Prussia emerges among the victors, gaining the Rhineland, Westphalia, northern Saxony and the Grand Duchy of Posen, restored as a leading great power.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a peace-settlement grant: PRU annexes/cores the Rhineland, Westphalia, northern Saxony and Posen regions; large prestige gain, stability restored, great-power status reaffirmed, and durable alliance/guarantee ties with RUS/AUS/GBR (the coalition victors).

result.notes: Rulers: Frederick II 'the Great' (b.1712, acc.1740, d.1786.8.17); Frederick William II (b.1744, acc.1786, d.1797.11.16); Frederick William III (b.1770, acc.1797, d.1840 — reigns through end of the 1815 window). Cross-power interactions are central: all three Poland partitions must be scripted as coordinated multi-tag events — 1772 = PRU+RUS+AUS, 1793 = PRU+RUS only (AUS excluded, distracted by the French war), 1795 = PRU+RUS+AUS erasing POL entirely; use a global-var/flag handshake so partners' annexations fire in sync. The Napoleonic arc is a reversal: PRU is knocked into a FRA-client state at Tilsit (1807), rebuilds via the reform era (1807-1812), then flips into the Russo-Prussian coalition (Kalisz 1813) — the neutrality flag (1795-1806) and client flag (1807-1813) are the key gates controlling when PRU may/must fight FRA. Territory bookkeeping: verify the mod has distinct states for Silesia, West Prussia/Pomerelia, Posen/South Prussia, Rhineland, Westphalia, and Saxony, and tags (or dynamic releases) for the Duchy of Warsaw and Kingdom of Westphalia to receive the Tilsit cessions. This is a text-only historical synthesis from established secondary history; I did not query internal Drive/Enterprise/GUS sources because the task is public well-documented history rather than Salesforce-internal research, and none of those sources would hold canonical Prussian history — flagging that as the one deliberate gap.

beats.event: The Peace of Hubertusburg ends the Seven Years' War with Austria failing to recover Silesia, confirming Prussia's permanent possession of the province.
beats.gameplay_effect: At game start lock Silesia region as PRU-owned/cored; fire a country_event that sets PRU as AUS rival, applies a one-time prestige penalty (-15) and a temporary 'lost_silesia' revanchism stability modifier; force peace (no active war vs PRU).

beats.event: Emperor Francis I Stephen (Maria Theresa's husband and Holy Roman Emperor) dies, and their son Joseph II is elected Holy Roman Emperor and made co-regent of the Habsburg lands.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire ruler-consort death event; install Joseph II (b.1741) as co-ruler/heir with an 'enlightened reformer' trait; grant AUS the Holy Roman Emperor title/diet leadership modifier; small prestige gain. Maria Theresa (b.1717, acc.1740) remains the reigning monarch.

beats.event: In the First Partition of Poland, Austria annexes Galicia and Lodomeria in coordination with Prussia and Russia.
beats.gameplay_effect: country_event annexes the Galicia region from POL to AUS (cede_state / add_core); simultaneously trigger parallel POL land-cessions to PRU and RUS so the partition is joint; grant AUS +10 prestige and a temporary non-aggression/coordination alliance flag with PRU and RUS.

beats.event: Maria Theresa dies and Joseph II becomes sole ruler, accelerating radical Enlightenment reforms (Edict of Toleration 1781, abolition of serfdom, centralization).
beats.gameplay_effect: Ruler death + succession to Joseph II (sole ruler 1780-1790); enact a 'Josephine reforms' centralization government modifier (+centralization/tax, -stability from noble/clergy unrest); apply small stability drop and prestige/legitimacy shift reflecting reform backlash.

beats.event: Joseph II dies embittered as revolts flare in the Austrian Netherlands and Hungary; his brother Leopold II succeeds and pragmatically reverses the most destabilizing reforms.
beats.gameplay_effect: Ruler death + succession to Leopold II (b.1747, r.1790-1792); fire event that removes the 'Josephine reforms' modifier, restores +stability, and quells revolt/separatism in the Austrian Netherlands and Hungarian states (suppress rebels, lower unrest).

beats.event: Leopold II dies on 1792.3.1 and is succeeded by his son Francis II (last Holy Roman Emperor); weeks later Revolutionary France declares war, opening the War of the First Coalition.
beats.gameplay_effect: Ruler death + succession to Francis II/I (b.1768); form defensive alliance with PRU; scripted FRA declare_war on AUS (First Coalition); set FRA as primary rival; apply mobilization/war-readiness modifier.

beats.event: The Third Partition of Poland erases the Polish state; Austria takes West Galicia including Kraków.
beats.gameplay_effect: country_event cedes West Galicia/Kraków states from POL to AUS with joint PRU+RUS partition events; POL is annexed/removed from the map; grant AUS +8 prestige.

beats.event: The Treaty of Campo Formio ends the First Coalition war: Austria cedes the Austrian Netherlands and Lombardy to France but is compensated with the territory of the Venetian Republic.
beats.gameplay_effect: Peace event: cede Austrian Netherlands (Belgium) and Lombardy to FRA; annex Venetia/Venice (VEN) to AUS as compensation; end AUS-FRA war; minor prestige loss offset by the Venetian gain.

beats.event: Anticipating Napoleon's imperial title, Francis proclaims himself Emperor of Austria (Francis I), and on 1806.8.6 he abdicates the crown and formally dissolves the Holy Roman Empire.
beats.gameplay_effect: Government/title change event: convert AUS to the 'Austrian Empire' (tag/name change, new imperial government form); on 1806.8.6 fire a second event that dissolves the HRE (release/detach the imperial subject-network and remove the Holy Roman Emperor title modifier); prestige gain from the new imperial title.

beats.event: Napoleon crushes the Austro-Russian army at Austerlitz; the Treaty of Pressburg forces Austria to cede Tyrol to Bavaria and Venetia to Napoleon's Kingdom of Italy.
beats.gameplay_effect: Scripted defeat in the Third Coalition war vs FRA: cede Tyrol to Bavaria (BAV) and cede Venetia to the Napoleonic Italian kingdom (ITA/FRA); large prestige loss (-20) and stability hit; break alliance with RUS.

beats.event: Austria's renewed war of the Fifth Coalition ends in defeat at Wagram; the Treaty of Schönbrunn strips Salzburg, the Illyrian Provinces (Adriatic coast) and West Galicia, and in 1810 Marie Louise is married to Napoleon to seal a Franco-Austrian alliance.
beats.gameplay_effect: Defeat event vs FRA: cede Illyria/Adriatic coast to FRA, Salzburg to BAV, and West Galicia to the Duchy of Warsaw (POL/WAR); then fire a 1810.4.2 royal-marriage + form-alliance event tying AUS to FRA (alliance flag, +opinion FRA, ends rivalry temporarily).

beats.event: Under Metternich, Austria abandons the French alliance to join the Sixth Coalition, helps defeat Napoleon at Leipzig, and in 1814-1815 hosts the Congress of Vienna, regaining Lombardy-Venetia, Tyrol, Galicia and Illyria and taking the presidency of the new German Confederation.
beats.gameplay_effect: Break alliance with FRA and declare_war on FRA; form coalition alliance with RUS, PRU and GBR; on victory fire a Congress-of-Vienna settlement event that returns Lombardy-Venetia, Tyrol, Salzburg, Galicia and Illyria to AUS, grants a large prestige boost (+30) and establishes AUS as leader/president of the German Confederation subject bloc.

result.notes: Rulers: Maria Theresa (b.1717, acc. Habsburg lands 1740, d.1780.11.29); Joseph II (b.1741, HRE 1765, co-regent 1765-1780, sole ruler 1780-1790, d.1790.2.20); Leopold II (b.1747, r.1790-1792, d.1792.3.1); Francis II/I (b.1768, HRE Francis II 1792-1806, Emperor of Austria Francis I from 1804, d.1835 — reigns through and past the 1815 endpoint). Cross-power interactions: both partitions modeled (First 1772, Third 1795 — note the Second Partition 1793 involved only PRU+RUS, NOT Austria, so no AUS gain there) require joint PRU+RUS cession events firing on POL simultaneously; the anti-French wars alternate AUS between coalition alliances (PRU/RUS/GBR) and, briefly 1810-1812, a Napoleonic alliance via the Marie Louise marriage. Klemens von Metternich became foreign minister in 1809 and is the architect of the 1813 realignment and the 1815 Congress. Optional extra beat if 12 is exceeded: War of the Bavarian Succession / 'Potato War' vs PRU 1778-1779, ended by the Treaty of Teschen 1779 with AUS gaining the small Innviertel — low-priority, can be a flavor event. Territorial caveat: Austrian Netherlands (Belgium) is lost for good at Campo Formio 1797 and is NOT restored to AUS at Vienna (traded for the enlarged Lombardy-Venetia), so the 1815 settlement event should grant northern Italy, not Belgium.

beats.event: Catherine II (b. 1729, seized the throne in the July 1762 coup that deposed her husband Peter III) rules an ascendant Russia as an enlightened autocrat at the game's start.
beats.gameplay_effect: On game start set RUS ruler to Catherine II with high stats and 'enlightened despot'/absolute-monarchy government; grant a stability boost (+2) and a large prestige floor; set AI aggression/expansion attitude high toward Ottomans (TUR) and Poland (POL); seed treasury and army bonuses.

beats.event: Russian bayonets secure the election of Catherine's former favorite Stanislaw August Poniatowski as King of Poland, turning the Commonwealth into a de facto Russian client.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire event making POL a Russian client_state/protectorate (create subject relationship RUS->POL) or install a pro-RUS ruler in POL; raise RUS influence/opinion in POL to max; prevent POL from allying rival great powers.

beats.event: The First Russo-Turkish War of Catherine's reign begins; Russian armies and the Mediterranean fleet crush Ottoman forces (Chesme 1770), ending in the Treaty of Kucuk Kaynarca (1774.7.21) with Black Sea gains and Crimean 'independence' from Istanbul.
beats.gameplay_effect: AI-autonomous scripted event: RUS declare_war on TUR with a conquest/coastal war goal; on victory transfer northern Black Sea coast provinces (Azov, Kinburn, Kerch) to RUS and release the Crimean Khanate as an independent RUS-aligned subject; grant prestige and war-score; +stability.

beats.event: Pugachev's Rebellion — a massive Cossack-and-peasant uprising in the Volga-Ural frontier claiming to be the dead Peter III — threatens the regime before being crushed by 1775.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a civil-war/rebellion event (start_civil_war or spawn rebel army stack) in RUS's southeastern provinces; apply temporary stability hit (-2) and manpower/unrest penalty; script auto-resolution suppressing the revolt within ~18 months, restoring stability and granting prestige on victory.

beats.event: The First Partition of Poland: Russia, Prussia and Austria jointly carve off nearly a third of the Commonwealth, Russia taking eastern Belarus and Polish Livonia.
beats.gameplay_effect: Cross-power scripted partition event coordinated among RUS+PRU+AUS: cede eastern Belarus/Latgale provinces from POL to RUS (and matching provinces to PRU and AUS); reduce POL size/prestige; grant RUS prestige; keep RUS-PRU-AUS non-aggression during the operation.

beats.event: Catherine formally annexes the Crimean Khanate, extinguishing the last vestige of Ottoman power on the northern Black Sea and founding the Black Sea Fleet.
beats.gameplay_effect: Scripted annex event: RUS annexes/integrates the Crimean Khanate subject (annex_subject / transfer all Crimean provinces to RUS); found a fleet (create navy at Sevastopol); grant prestige; raise threat/opinion penalty with TUR to prime the next war.

beats.event: The Second Russo-Turkish War (1787-1792) confirms the Crimean annexation and, by the Treaty of Jassy, pushes the border to the Dniester with the acquisition of Yedisan and the fortress of Ochakov.
beats.gameplay_effect: AI event: TUR (or RUS) declare_war; on RUS victory transfer the Yedisan/Ochakov provinces between the Bug and Dniester to RUS; grant war-score prestige; brief alliance/co-belligerence with AUS against TUR during the war.

beats.event: The Second Partition of Poland (Russia and Prussia only, Austria excluded) strips away most of the remaining Commonwealth in reprisal for the reformist May Constitution.
beats.gameplay_effect: Cross-power event RUS+PRU: cede central-Belarus/Right-bank Ukraine provinces from POL to RUS and Danzig/Greater Poland to PRU; AUS NOT involved this time; large POL prestige/size collapse; RUS prestige gain.

beats.event: The Third Partition (Russia, Prussia, Austria) erases Poland-Lithuania from the map entirely after the failed Kosciuszko Uprising.
beats.gameplay_effect: Final tripartite partition event: transfer all remaining POL provinces among RUS (Lithuania/Courland/western Ukraine), PRU and AUS, then delete/annex the POL tag so it ceases to exist; suppress the Kosciuszko rebel stacks; grant RUS major prestige.

beats.event: Catherine the Great dies of a stroke; her estranged son Paul I (b. 1754) accedes and reverses her policies with erratic, pro-Prussian and eventually pro-Napoleon diplomacy.
beats.gameplay_effect: Ruler death + succession event: kill Catherine II, crown Paul I with lower diplomatic/erratic stats; apply a stability dip (-1) and a legitimacy/opinion penalty with nobility; swing RUS AI attitude away from Britain/Austria toward rapprochement with France.

beats.event: Paul I is assassinated in a palace coup by disaffected nobles and officers; his son Alexander I (b. 1777) takes the throne amid reformist hopes.
beats.gameplay_effect: Assassination/succession event: kill Paul I via a noble-conspiracy event, crown Alexander I with high diplomatic stats; brief stability shock then recovery; reset RUS AI toward anti-French coalition alignment (opinion boost with GBR/AUS).

beats.event: Russia joins the Third Coalition and is decisively beaten alongside Austria by Napoleon at Austerlitz; after Friedland (1807) the Treaty of Tilsit forces alliance with France and entry into the Continental System.
beats.gameplay_effect: Two-stage event: RUS+AUS declare_war/join war vs France (FRA), suffer a scripted defeat (war-score loss, prestige/stability hit at Austerlitz/Friedland); then Tilsit event forms a forced RUS-FRA alliance and trade-embargo modifier vs GBR; concurrently RUS declare_war on Sweden (SWE) and annex Finland by the 1809 Treaty of Fredrikshamn.

beats.event: Napoleon invades Russia with the Grande Armee; after Borodino and the burning of Moscow the French army is annihilated in the winter retreat, breaking Napoleon's power.
beats.gameplay_effect: Scripted invasion event: break the RUS-FRA alliance and have FRA declare_war on RUS with a huge invading army; script scorched-earth attrition and winter modifiers that destroy the French stacks; on the repulse grant RUS massive prestige and war-score and a stability/manpower boost.

beats.event: Leading the Sixth Coalition, Alexander I's armies advance across Germany, win at Leipzig (1813), and Russian troops enter Paris, ending Napoleon's empire.
beats.gameplay_effect: Scripted counter-offensive: RUS forms/leads a coalition alliance with PRU+AUS+GBR vs FRA, occupies French territory (capital occupation event 'March on Paris'), forces FRA capitulation; grant RUS peak prestige.

beats.event: At the Congress of Vienna Russia emerges as the dominant continental power: a 'Congress Poland' kingdom is created under the Russian tsar and Alexander founds the Holy Alliance.
beats.gameplay_effect: Settlement event: create/attach a Congress Poland subject under RUS (restore POL as a RUS royal-union/personal-union vassal holding the Warsaw provinces) and confirm Finland/Bessarabia holdings; form the 'Holy Alliance' defensive pact with PRU+AUS; set RUS prestige/great-power rank to the highest in Europe.

result.notes: Rulers: Catherine II 'the Great' (b. 1729-05-02, acceded 1762-07-09 via coup vs Peter III, d. 1796-11-17); Paul I (b. 1754-10-01, acceded 1796-11-17, assassinated 1801-03-23); Alexander I (b. 1777-12-23, acceded 1801-03-23, d. 1825 — outside window). Cross-power interactions: all three Polish partitions are JOINT operations — 1772 = RUS+PRU+AUS, 1793 = RUS+PRU only (Austria excluded), 1795 = RUS+PRU+AUS (finishing POL off). The Napoleonic arc requires coordination with FRA, PRU, AUS and GBR tags: Tilsit (1807) briefly allies RUS+FRA, the 1812 invasion breaks it, and 1813-15 flips RUS into leading the anti-French coalition. Note the Finnish War (1808-09 vs SWE) runs concurrently with the French alliance and is folded into the 1805/Tilsit beat for compactness. If the mod wants more granularity, split out Chesme (1770), Borodino (1812-09-07) and Leipzig (1813-10) as sub-events. Caveat: exact province tags depend on the mod's 1763 map — the effects name historical regions (Yedisan, Ochakov, Latgale, Congress Poland) that must be mapped to the mod's province IDs. I did not have mod files or GUS items to cross-reference; this is drawn from standard historiography, so validate province/subject-tag availability against the mod's actual 1763 setup before wiring events.

beats.event: The Treaty of Paris ends the Seven Years' War in which Bourbon Spain fought alongside France; Spain cedes Florida to Britain but keeps Louisiana, which France secretly transferred to it at Fontainebleau in 1762.
beats.gameplay_effect: At game start (fired ~1763.2.16) confirm SPA is allied to FRA via a scripted add_alliance/Family-Compact flag; cede the Florida region/provinces to GBR and grant own_control_core of the Louisiana region to SPA; apply a small negative prestige hit for the war loss and set a country flag spa_family_compact = yes.

beats.event: Charles III (b.1716, acceded 1759) drives the Bourbon Reforms — centralizing the empire, intendancy system, free-trade decrees, and army/navy rebuilding to prepare for revenge on Britain.
beats.gameplay_effect: Fire a repeating/one-shot reform pulse for SPA: add_stability, add_prestige, gain a centralization/administrative-efficiency modifier and a treasury/economy boost; nudge AI research/military-tradition investment; set flag spa_bourbon_reforms = yes.

beats.event: Charles III expels the Jesuit order from Spain and all its overseas dominions in a single royal decree.
beats.gameplay_effect: One-shot event: apply a temporary stability dip then a lasting crown-authority/secular-power modifier (reduced clergy influence, higher monarch power); log via se_LOG. Minor prestige gain for royal assertiveness.

beats.event: Under the Treaty of Aranjuez Spain joins France in the American Revolutionary War against Britain and lays siege to Gibraltar.
beats.gameplay_effect: AI-autonomous event: SPA declare_war on GBR (join FRA's war), confirm alliance with FRA, and open a siege/wargoal on the Gibraltar province plus Minorca and West Florida; small prestige/aggression boost so the AI commits fleets and armies.

beats.event: The Treaty of Paris/Versailles ends the war: Spain regains East and West Florida and the island of Menorca from Britain.
beats.gameplay_effect: On war resolution grant SPA the Florida region (East+West) and Menorca province via own_control_core transfer from GBR; add_prestige and add_stability for the successful revanche; end the GBR war and keep the FRA alliance.

beats.event: Charles III dies; his son Charles IV (b.1748) accedes but is a weak ruler dominated after 1792 by favorite Manuel Godoy.
beats.gameplay_effect: Ruler death + succession event: kill the Charles III character and install Charles IV as monarch with lower skill stats; spawn/raise Godoy as a dominant minister character; apply a legitimacy/prestige dip and reduced ruler-competence modifier.

beats.event: After the execution of Louis XVI, Spain joins the First Coalition and fights the War of the Pyrenees (War of the Convention) against Revolutionary France.
beats.gameplay_effect: AI-autonomous event: break the SPA-FRA alliance, SPA declare_war on FRA (Revolutionary France tag) with a Pyrenees frontier wargoal; brief prestige/stability bump for defending the monarchy, then set flag spa_at_war_france = yes.

beats.event: The Peace of Basel ends the losing war; Spain cedes its two-thirds of Hispaniola (Santo Domingo) to France.
beats.gameplay_effect: On resolution SPA cede the Santo Domingo province(s) to FRA and end the war; apply negative prestige and a small stability hit reflecting the defeat; clear spa_at_war_france.

beats.event: The Second Treaty of San Ildefonso reverses alliances: Spain allies Revolutionary France against Britain, dragging its fleet into the wider war.
beats.gameplay_effect: AI-autonomous diplomacy event: form alliance with FRA, then SPA declare_war on GBR; set flag spa_franco_spanish_alliance = yes; queue naval mobilization so the AI keeps ships near the fleet-in-being for the coming combined operations.

beats.event: The Third Treaty of San Ildefonso returns Louisiana to Napoleonic France (which sells it to the United States in 1803).
beats.gameplay_effect: Scripted cession: transfer the Louisiana region from SPA to FRA (own_control_core); optionally chain a 1803 hand-off of that region toward a USA tag if present. Minor prestige loss; log the transfer.

beats.event: At the Battle of Trafalgar the British fleet under Nelson destroys the combined Franco-Spanish fleet off Cadiz, ending Spain's naval power.
beats.gameplay_effect: AI-autonomous naval-defeat event: destroy/disband the bulk of SPA's navy (kill the standing fleet units), apply a large naval-morale and prestige penalty and a lasting reduced-naval-capacity modifier; set flag spa_trafalgar_lost = yes.

beats.event: The Mutiny of Aranjuez forces Charles IV to abdicate to Ferdinand VII (b.1784); at Bayonne Napoleon forces both to renounce the throne and installs his brother Joseph Bonaparte as King of Spain.
beats.gameplay_effect: Government-change/puppet event: remove Charles IV and Ferdinand VII as rulers, change_government to a French-installed regime (Joseph Bonaparte monarch), make SPA a subject/puppet of FRA; large stability crash and legitimacy collapse; set flag spa_bonaparte_king = yes.

beats.event: The Dos de Mayo uprising in Madrid ignites the Peninsular War, a nationwide insurrection and guerrilla war against French occupation, backed by Britain and Portugal.
beats.gameplay_effect: Trigger a civil-war/revolt: spawn a patriot 'Junta/Cadiz Cortes' faction (or start_civil_war) fighting the Bonaparte regime; SPA-patriot side allies GBR and POR, declares/continues war on FRA; heavy unrest, guerrilla attrition modifier on French-held provinces.

beats.event: The Anglo-Spanish-Portuguese army under Wellington shatters the French at Vitoria, and the Treaty of Valençay (Dec 1813) sees Napoleon recognize Ferdinand VII.
beats.gameplay_effect: On patriot victory, drive FRA armies out of Iberia (transfer/liberate occupied SPA provinces back to the patriot tag), break SPA's subject status to FRA, restore full sovereignty; add_prestige and begin stability recovery.

beats.event: Ferdinand VII returns to Spain, is restored to the throne, and in May abolishes the liberal 1812 Constitution to rule as an absolute monarch.
beats.gameplay_effect: Restoration event: install Ferdinand VII as monarch, change_government back to absolute Bourbon monarchy, apply an absolutist/reactionary modifier (reduced liberal factions, higher crown authority); moderate stability gain but a latent unrest/colonial-revolt modifier reflecting the coming Spanish American wars of independence; set flag spa_ferdinand_restored = yes.

result.notes:
Rulers: Charles III (b.1716, acceded 1759, d.1788.12.14) — reformer, architect of the Bourbon reforms and the 1779 war on Britain; Charles IV (b.1748, acceded 1788, forced abdication 1808, d.1819) — weak, dominated by favorite Manuel Godoy from 1792; Ferdinand VII (b.1784, brief accession Mar 1808, deposed at Bayonne, restored 1814, d.1833) — restored absolutist. Godoy is a key non-royal power-broker character worth scripting for 1792-1808.

Cross-power interactions: Spain's whole arc is Bourbon-Compact-with-FRA vs GBR — alliance with FRA at start, joins FRA in 1779 American Revolution alongside the nascent USA, breaks and fights Revolutionary FRA 1793-95, re-allies FRA 1796, then is invaded and puppeted by Napoleonic FRA 1808. Peninsular War (1808-1814) requires GBR + POR as co-belligerents on the patriot side; Wellington's Anglo-Portuguese army is the historical liberating force. The 1800 Louisiana cession chains SPA->FRA->USA (Louisiana Purchase 1803). Spain was NOT a partition-of-Poland participant (that was PRU+AUS+RUS); mentioned only as the prompt's cross-power example.

Caveats: Exact Treaty of Paris date is 1763.2.10, six days before the 1763.2.16 game start, so the Florida-cede / Louisiana-gain should be baked into the 1763 setup or fired as a turn-1 event. Florida return in 1783 covers both East and West Florida plus Menorca (Gibraltar siege failed — do NOT grant Gibraltar). The 1795 Basel cession is Santo Domingo (eastern Hispaniola) only. Post-1814 latent instability (Latin American independence wars, 1820 Riego liberal revolt) is beyond the 1815 focus window but should be seeded as a modifier for continuity.
